lines: Carter is quietly becoming the most annoyingly well-positioned cunt in this sweepstake — Algeria drew 3-3, England smashed Panama, DR Congo won, and Colombia held Portugal; mate's got fingers in every pie and somehow none of them are on fire yet.

Ellis, you absolute fuck — Argentina are cruising through as group winners and you're sitting there with a front-row seat to Messi's farewell tour while everyone else scrambles; enjoy it while South Korea and Paraguay slowly decompose around you.

Croft's Norway shares are propping up his entire operation because Uruguay are a shambles on 2 points and Uzbekistan just got battered 3-1 by DR Congo, which is the kind of result that makes you question all your life choices.
Wilmot has four teams in the sweepstake and somehow Saudi Arabia — sitting on 2 points with zero wins — is the one that best represents his overall contribution to this group chat.

Today's fixture: South Africa vs Canada could decide Group A and Group B fates simultaneously — Jim needs Qatar to somehow not be eliminated already, and Ellis is praying South Korea can scrape through, so watch this space for maximum chaos.
